tactic: Loss frame -> 'now 137K have' punch -> mechanism reveal -> recursion
primary_text:
Before 2018, only the wealthy could invest in an Australian startup. Now 137,000 Australians have.

The law changed. Crowd-Sourced Funding came in. We built the rails. 324 raises since: Naked Life, Our Cow, Earthletica, Aquafab.

Now we are opening our own raise on the platform we built.

Save your spot. We will tell you the moment the offer goes live.

*Always consider the general CSF risk warning and offer document before investing.
headline: What only VCs used to access
description: 324 raises. $234M+. 137,000 retail investors. Save your spot.
_source_label: DRC V3 -- A3 Empathy + Access + Recursion (first-timers)
_v3_changes: Hook rewrite: 'Until 2018, backing the next big Aussie brand was for VCs and family offices.' -> 'Before 2018, only the wealthy could invest in an Australian startup. Now 137,000 Australians have.' Sharper antagonist ('only the wealthy'), then community FOMO punch in line 2 (137K already crossed the line). Halbert 4->5.
Body restructured into three short sentences: law changed, mechanism, we built it.
Headline retouched: 'What VCs got, you can get too' -> 'What only VCs used to access' (sharper, drops the abstract 'too').
_drc_audience: B2C considered, solution-aware. First-time CSF investors who feel locked out of startup equity.
_compliance_findings_resolved: PREOFFER-VERBS: 'save your spot'. 'Invest' appears in past-tense factual context ('only the wealthy could invest before 2018'), not as a current call to action.
VERBATIM-OFFER-LANGUAGE: 'we will tell you the moment the offer goes live'.
